mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate cuts over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market health and reduced consumer spending, triggering immediate negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict downturns during documented labor market soft patches.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving sustained positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to precede positive quarterly SPX returns in > 60% of historical cases.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average indicates supply-side weakness that compresses corporate earnings expectations, yielding negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or flat during industrial production contractions, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation growth slows below 0.1% monthly while the federal funds rate remains above 4.0%, markets price in imminent monetary easing, generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of low-inflation months, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a buy-and-hold baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Declining nonfarm payrolls growth reduces aggregate income and corporate revenue projections, systematically lowering S&P 500 valuations over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly SPX returns show no negative correlation with payroll declines, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth combined with accelerating retail sales confirms robust domestic demand, which lifts equity risk premiums and produces positive monthly forward returns.
failure_condition: Concurrent GDP and retail sales growth fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of observations, or the interaction term loses significance in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial output and rising housing inventory signals overcapacity and weak demand absorption, triggering immediate negative equity repricing.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns during supply-demand divergence episodes, or the mechanism fails to predict drawdowns when credit spreads widen.
